QF.id: VI Functions of DP Account Operations_12

question.text: Communications about a joint demat account (dividends, annual reports, meeting notices) are sent to:

answer.text: The joint holder with the highest PAN number
Any one joint holder chosen at account opening

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Communications about the joint depository account are provided only to the first holder. Dividend/interest warrants, annual reports, and meeting notices go to the first-named holder. **The Trap:** 'All joint holders individually' would be ideal but is not the practice — only the first holder receives communications. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series V-A covers communication protocols for joint MF holdings. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** This is why the order of names matters in a joint account — the first holder is the primary contact person for all communications.
